title: RapidMeta Endocrinology | Teplizumab for Delay of Stage 3 Type 1 Diabetes v1.0

pico.pop: Non-diabetic relatives (>=8 years old) of patients with type 1 diabetes who have >=2 diabetes-related autoantibodies and abnormal glucose tolerance (Stage 2 T1D pre-clinical)
pico.int: Teplizumab 14-day single-course IV infusion (anti-CD3 humanised monoclonal antibody, dose-escalating to 826 ug/m^2/day)
pico.out: Time to clinical (Stage 3) type 1 diabetes diagnosis (primary; HR vs placebo)
pico.subgroup: Age (<18 vs >=18), HLA-DR3/DR4 status, baseline OGTT severity, autoantibody profile

real_data_entries.name: TN-10
real_data_entries.group: Stage 2 (pre-clinical) T1D in autoantibody-positive relatives, teplizumab 14-day course vs placebo (time to Stage 3 T1D diagnosis HR)

allOutcomes.title: Time to clinical (Stage 3) type 1 diabetes diagnosis (primary; HR vs placebo)
C-peptide AUC change at 6 months (relative preservation, MD vs placebo)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Herold KC et al. N Engl J Med 2019;381:603-613 (TN-10). Long-term extension: Sims EK et al. Sci Transl Med 2021.
